question: Explain the role of the CVCS (Chemical and Volume Control System).

nuscale_ch01_0361.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.5 Chemical and Volume Control System Design and Arrangement
nuscale_ch01_0361.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), noting that the design generally meets requirements, with some exceptions due to differences in system design (e.g., absence of holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions). The report confirms compliance or partial compliance with relevant DSRS sections, and clarifies that certain criteria are not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0364.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.8 ITAAC
nuscale_ch01_0364.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System. It confirms that the NuScale design generally meets the requirements of DSRS 9.3.4, with the exception of certain criteria related to holdup tanks, which are not applicable to the NuScale design. The section also notes that the relevant Inspections, Tests, Analyses, and Acceptance Criteria (ITAAC) are met, and clarifies that some referenced standards apply only to other reactor types.

nuscale_ch01_0366.title: DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0: Containment Evacuation and Flooding SystemsII.1 GDC 2
nuscale_ch01_0366.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for containment evacuation and flooding systems, specifically addressing GDC 2. It states that the NuScale design meets the requirements of DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0, with no exceptions or additional notes.

nuscale_ch01_0363.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.7 Prevention of CVCS Holdup Tank Wall Buckling/Failure; CVCS Venting and Draining
nuscale_ch01_0363.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) partially conforms to the regulatory criterion regarding prevention of holdup tank wall buckling/failure and venting/draining. The partial conformance is due to differences in the NuScale design, which does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions, but the system will still provide appropriate venting and draining capabilities as required.

nuscale_ch01_0047.title: Annex Building
nuscale_ch01_0047.summary: The Annex Building (ANB) is a nonsafety-related structure in the NuScale Power Plant that serves multiple functions, including controlling access to radiologically and non-radiologically controlled areas, providing personnel support services (such as locker rooms, showers, and first aid), and housing decontamination equipment and security support facilities. It plays a key role in supporting plant operations and security but is not classified as safety-related.

nuscale_ch01_0359.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.3 Minimization of contamination
nuscale_ch01_0359.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System. It details which aspects of the Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design-Specific Review Standard (DSRS) are met, partially met, or not applicable, particularly focusing on contamination minimization, sampling capabilities, system functional design, and safety-related functions such as preventing inadvertent boron dilution. The report notes full or partial compliance with relevant criteria, with some exceptions due to design differences or regulatory updates.

nuscale_ch01_0357.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.1 CVCS Functional Performance during Adverse Environmental Phenomena; Pumping Capacity; and defense-in-depth RCS makeup
nuscale_ch01_0357.summary: This section reviews the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) and related systems for conformance with regulatory criteria. It notes that the CVCS partially conforms to requirements for functional performance during adverse environmental conditions, with its only safety-related function being the prevention of inadvertent boron dilution in the reactor coolant system. The system fully conforms to single failure criteria, which apply specifically to the safety-related demineralized water isolation valves.

nuscale_ch01_0045.title: Other Site Structures
nuscale_ch01_0045.summary: This section describes the functions and characteristics of several nonsafety-related site structures at a NuScale Power Plant, including the Turbine Generator Buildings (TGBs), Annex Building (ANB), and Security Buildings (SCBs). The TGBs house turbine generators and maintenance facilities, the ANB provides personnel support and security functions, and the SCBs manage site access and security operations.

nuscale_ch01_0358.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.2 Single Failure Criteria and GDC 5
nuscale_ch01_0358.summary: This section states that the NuScale Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) conforms to the single failure criteria and General Design Criterion (GDC) 5, specifically for the two safety-related demineralized water isolation valves that prevent inadvertent boron dilution of the reactor coolant. The single-failure criteria are only applicable to these valves, ensuring the system's safety function is maintained.
